claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 12 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 12 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you're looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I can claim Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex, who is 12 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great to meet you and hear about Alex.

Now, I have a few more questions to help determine your eligibility:

Does Alex live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you.

Is Alex a UK resident, and are you also living in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK, and Alex lives here with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Wonderful, thank you for confirming that.

Next, could you tell me about Alex's education or training status? Is Alex currently in full-time education, or are they under 16 and not yet in that stage of schooling?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 12, so they're still in school - just regular secondary school at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Since Alex is 12 and in regular secondary school, that's straightforward.

Just a couple more questions:

Are you responsible for Alex's day-to-day care? In other words, are you the person who looks after Alex and is responsible for their upbringing?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm the one looking after Alex day-to-day - I take care of everything they need.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you.

One more question: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me summarise your situation:
- You live in the UK
- Alex is 12 years old and lives with you
- Alex is in full-time education (secondary school)
- You are responsible for Alex's day-to-day care
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you should be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

- ✅ **Age**: Alex is 12 years old, which is under 16
- ✅ **Residency**: Both you and Alex live in the UK
- ✅ **Responsibility**: You are responsible for Alex as they live with you and you take care of their day-to-day needs
- ✅ **Education**: Alex is in full-time education at secondary school
- ✅ **No duplicate claims**: No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit. Is there anything else you'd like to know about the process?

reasonings: Alex is 12 years old, which is under 16, so they meet the age requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant lives in the UK and Alex lives with them, which satisfies the residency requirement. The claimant is responsible for Alex's day-to-day care as they live together and look after all of Alex's needs. Alex is in approved education (secondary school). No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. They are a UK resident who is responsible for a child under 16 (Alex, aged 12) who lives with them. Alex is in full-time education, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for this child. The claimant should be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

The claimant lives in the UK and Alex lives with them, which satisfies the residency requirement. The claimant is responsible for Alex's day-to-day care as they live together and look after all of Alex's needs. Alex is in approved education (secondary school). No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. They are a UK resident who is responsible for a child under 16 (Alex, aged 12) who lives with them. Alex is in full-time education, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for this child. The claimant should be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
